{{Quote|There's a colony of living, eating sand all along the shoreline…|Tinian I'att describes the coastlines of Lomabu III|<ref name="Pelt" />}}
The '''Lomabuans''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that were native to the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lomabu III]]. The species constructed [[City/Legends|cities]] of [[Steel/Legends|steel]] and stone on the coastlines of the planet's archipelagoes. However, during the rule of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the Lomabuans were held to be seditious, and by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] Lomabu III was entirely depopulated. The ruins of the Lomabuan civilization slowly dissolved into the surrounding oceans.

==Biology and appearance==
The Lomabuans were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. [[Chenlambec]], a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who visited their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], was unaware of what the Lomabuans looked like.<ref name="Pelt" />

==Society and culture==
The Lomabuans developed a civilization on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lomabu III]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], constructing [[City/Legends|cities]] of stone and [[Steel/Legends|steel]] on the edge of the world's archipelagoes. The Lomabuans decorated the walls of those cities with filigree. By 3 ABY, those cities were partially submerged underneath an [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]], and [[Color/Legends|green]] [[Algae/Legends|algae]] grew over many of the ruined structures.<ref name="Pelt" />

==History==
The Lomabuans were native to Lomabu III, one of six planets in the [[Lomabu system/Legends|Lomabu system]], and a world that was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by a single [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. The Lomabuan's homeworld was covered almost entirely by [[Salt/Legends|salt]] [[Water/Legends|water]] oceans, apart from several long archipelagoes that breached the ocean's surface, tracing arcs where the planet's tectonic plates collided. Along the coastlines of those islands, which were covered in jungles<ref name="Pelt" /> and swamps,<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> the Lomabuans established settlements. The species shared their homeworld with colonies of tiny [[Sand|creatures]]—each no larger than a grain of sand—that lived on the edge of the tidal zone and devoured anything with which they came into contact.<ref name="Pelt" />

[[File:Chenlambec.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Chenlambec visited Lomabu III.]]
Located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Aida sector/Legends|Aida sector]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> the region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] surrounding the Lomabuan's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was explored by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] between [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]] and [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]]. The Lomabuan's homeworld remained within Republic territory through many [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflicts, although during the [[Jedi Civil War]], it was just outside the farthest extent of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], and during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], it bordered the expanse of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the Republic was reformed into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Atlas" /> By 3 ABY, the Lomabuans were considered seditious by the Empire; the Lomabuan cities were ruined and the entire population forcibly depopulated from Lomabu III. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], their homeworld was used as the location of a [[Lomabu III penal colony|penal colony]] for Wookiees by the Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of the [[Aida sector/Legends|Aida sector]], [[Io Desnand]]. The Wookiees were [[Mission to Lomabu III|freed]] by the bounty hunter Chenlambec, who, while on Lomabu III, wondered whether the Lomabuans were [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] somewhere or were all [[Death/Legends|dead]] and speculated that within decades, the oceans would have eroded all trace of the Lomabuan civilization.<ref name="Pelt" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Kathy Tyers]] created the Lomabuans for her short story ''[[The Prize Pelt: The Tale of Bossk]]'', which was published in [[Bantam Spectra]]'s ''[[Tales of the Bounty Hunters]]'' anthology in [[1996]].<ref name="Pelt" />
